1: //! Postgres database layer for Automaton.
2: //! All SQL operations go through this crate — modules, flows, jobs, secrets, triggers, graph.
3: 
4: use sqlx::postgres::{PgPool, PgPoolOptions};
5: use sqlx::Row;
6: 
7: pub struct AutomatonDb {
8:     pool: PgPool,
9: }
10: 
11: impl AutomatonDb {
12:     /// Connect to Postgres and run migrations.
13:     pub async fn connect(database_url: &str) -> Result<Self, sqlx::Error> {
14:         let pool = PgPoolOptions::new()
15:             .max_connections(20)
16:             .connect(database_url)
17:             .await?;
18:         let db = Self { pool };
19:         db.migrate().await?;
20:         Ok(db)
21:     }
22: 
23:     async fn migrate(&self) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
24:         sqlx::query(
25:             r#"
26:             CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS scripts (
27:                 hash TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
28:                 path TEXT NOT NULL,
29:                 version TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '0.1.0',
30:                 source TEXT NOT NULL,
31:                 manifest JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
32:                 parent_hash TEXT REFERENCES scripts(hash),
33:                 built BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
34:                 created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
35:             );
36:             CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS script_deps (
37:                 script_hash TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES scripts(hash) ON DELETE CASCADE,
38:                 depends_on TEXT NOT NULL,
39:                 version_req TEXT,
40:                 PRIMARY KEY (script_hash, depends_on)
41:             );
42:             CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS flows (
43:                 hash TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
44:                 path TEXT NOT NULL,
45:                 version TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '0.1.0',
46:                 flow_definition JSONB NOT NULL,
47:                 parent_hash TEXT REFERENCES flows(hash),
48:                 created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
49:             );
50:             CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS jobs (
51:                 id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
52:                 kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'script',
53:                 target_path TEXT NOT NULL,
54:                 args JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
55:                 scheduled_for TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
56:                 priority INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
57:                 running BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
58:                 worker_id TEXT,
59:                 created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
60:             );
61:             CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS runs (
62:                 id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
63:                 target_path TEXT NOT NULL,
64:                 kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'script',
65:                 args JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
66:                 result JSONB,
67:                 error TEXT,
68:                 state TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
69:                 attempt INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
70:                 duration_ms BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
71:                 created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
72:                 completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ
73:             );
74:             CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS graph_nodes (
75:                 id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
76:                 kind TEXT NOT NULL,
77:                 name TEXT NOT NULL,
78:                 properties JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
79:                 created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
80:             );
81:             CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS graph_edges (
82:                 id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
83:                 source TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES graph_nodes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
84:                 target TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES graph_nodes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
85:                 kind TEXT NOT NULL,
86:                 properties JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
87:                 created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
88:             );
89:             CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS variables (
90:                 path TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
91:                 value TEXT NOT NULL,
92:                 is_secret BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
93:                 description TEXT,
94:                 created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
95:             );
96:             CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS resources (
97:                 path TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
98:                 resource_type TEXT NOT NULL,
99:                 value JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
100:                 description TEXT,
101:                 created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
102:             );
103:             CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS triggers (
104:                 id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
105:                 target_path TEXT NOT NULL,
106:                 target_is_flow BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
107:                 trigger_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'cron',
108:                 config JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
109:                 enabled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
110:                 created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
111:             );
112:             CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_scripts_path ON scripts(path);
113:             CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_jobs_scheduled ON jobs(scheduled_for) WHERE NOT running;
114:             CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_runs_target ON runs(target_path);
115:             CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_runs_state ON runs(state);
116:             CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_graph_nodes_kind ON graph_nodes(kind);
117:             CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_graph_edges_source ON graph_edges(source);
118:             CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_triggers_enabled ON triggers(enabled);
119:             "#,
120:         )
121:         .execute(&self.pool)
122:         .await?;
123:         Ok(())
124:     }
125: 
126:     // ── Script operations ──
127: 
128:     pub async fn register_script(
129:         &self,
130:         path: &str,
131:         source: &str,
132:         version: &str,
133:         manifest: &serde_json::Value,
134:         deps: &[automaton_core::DepRef],
135:     ) -> Result<String, sqlx::Error> {
136:         use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};
137:         let hash = format!("{:x}", Sha256::digest(source.as_bytes()));
138:         sqlx::query(
139:             "INSERT INTO scripts (hash, path, version, source, manifest) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5)
140:              ON CONFLICT (hash) DO UPDATE SET path = EXCLUDED.path",
141:         )
142:         .bind(&hash)
143:         .bind(path)
144:         .bind(version)
145:         .bind(source)
146:         .bind(manifest)
147:         .execute(&self.pool)
148:         .await?;
149:         for dep in deps {
150:             sqlx::query(
151:                 "INSERT INTO script_deps (script_hash, depends_on, version_req) VALUES ($1, $2, $3)
152:                  ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING",
153:             )
154:             .bind(&hash)
155:             .bind(&dep.name)
156:             .bind(&dep.version_req)
157:             .execute(&self.pool)
158:             .await?;
159:         }
160:         Ok(hash)
161:     }
162: 
163:     pub async fn get_script(&self, path: &str) -> Result<Option<serde_json::Value>, sqlx::Error> {
164:         let row = sqlx::query(
165:             "SELECT hash, path, version, source, manifest, built, created_at FROM scripts WHERE path = $1 ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1",
166:         )
167:         .bind(path)
168:         .fetch_optional(&self.pool)
169:         .await?;
170:         Ok(row.map(|r| {
171:             serde_json::json!({
172:                 "hash": r.get::<String, _>("hash"),
173:                 "path": r.get::<String, _>("path"),
174:                 "version": r.get::<String, _>("version"),
175:                 "source": r.get::<String, _>("source"),
176:                 "manifest": r.get::<serde_json::Value, _>("manifest"),
177:                 "built": r.get::<bool, _>("built"),
178:                 "created_at": r.get::<chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>, _>("created_at"),
179:             })
180:         }))
181:     }
182: 
183:     pub async fn list_scripts(&self) -> Result<Vec<serde_json::Value>, sqlx::Error> {
184:         let rows = sqlx::query(
185:             "SELECT DISTINCT ON (path) hash, path, version, built, created_at FROM scripts ORDER BY path, created_at DESC",
186:         )
187:         .fetch_all(&self.pool)
188:         .await?;
189:         Ok(rows.into_iter().map(|r| {
190:             serde_json::json!({
191:                 "hash": r.get::<String, _>("hash"),
192:                 "path": r.get::<String, _>("path"),
193:                 "version": r.get::<String, _>("version"),
194:                 "built": r.get::<bool, _>("built"),
195:             })
196:         }).collect())
197:     }
198: 
199:     pub async fn mark_built(&self, path: &str) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
200:         sqlx::query("UPDATE scripts SET built = true WHERE path = $1")
201:             .bind(path)
202:             .execute(&self.pool)
203:             .await?;
204:         Ok(())
205:     }
206: 
207:     // ── Job queue operations ──
208: 
209:     pub async fn enqueue(&self, kind: &str, target: &str, args: &serde_json::Value) -> Result<i64, sqlx::Error> {
210:         let row = sqlx::query(
211:             "INSERT INTO jobs (kind, target_path, args) VALUES ($1, $2, $3) RETURNING id",
212:         )
213:         .bind(kind)
214:         .bind(target)
215:         .bind(args)
216:         .fetch_one(&self.pool)
217:         .await?;
218:         Ok(row.get::<i64, _>("id"))
219:     }
220: 
221:     pub async fn dequeue(&self, worker_id: &str) -> Result<Option<serde_json::Value>, sqlx::Error> {
222:         let row = sqlx::query(
223:             "UPDATE jobs SET running = true, worker_id = $1
224:              WHERE id = (
225:                  SELECT id FROM jobs WHERE NOT running AND scheduled_for <= NOW()
226:                  ORDER BY priority DESC, created_at ASC LIMIT 1
227:                  FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED
228:              )
229:              RETURNING id, kind, target_path, args, scheduled_for, priority",
230:         )
231:         .bind(worker_id)
232:         .fetch_optional(&self.pool)
233:         .await?;
234:         Ok(row.map(|r| {
235:             serde_json::json!({
236:                 "id": r.get::<i64, _>("id"),
237:                 "kind": r.get::<String, _>("kind"),
238:                 "target_path": r.get::<String, _>("target_path"),
239:                 "args": r.get::<serde_json::Value, _>("args"),
240:                 "scheduled_for": r.get::<chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>, _>("scheduled_for"),
241:                 "priority": r.get::<i32, _>("priority"),
242:             })
243:         }))
244:     }
245: 
246:     pub async fn complete_job(&self, job_id: i64, _run_id: &str) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
247:         sqlx::query("DELETE FROM jobs WHERE id = $1")
248:             .bind(job_id)
249:             .execute(&self.pool)
250:             .await?;
251:         Ok(())
252:     }
253: 
254:     pub async fn list_jobs(&self, limit: i64) -> Result<Vec<serde_json::Value>, sqlx::Error> {
255:         let rows = sqlx::query(
256:             "SELECT id, kind, target_path, args, scheduled_for, priority, running, worker_id
257:              FROM jobs ORDER BY priority DESC, scheduled_for ASC LIMIT $1")
258:             .bind(limit)
259:             .fetch_all(&self.pool)
260:             .await?;
261:         Ok(rows.iter().map(|r| {
262:             serde_json::json!({
263:                 "id": r.get::<i64, _>("id"),
264:                 "kind": r.get::<String, _>("kind"),
265:                 "target_path": r.get::<String, _>("target_path"),
266:                 "scheduled_for": r.get::<chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>, _>("scheduled_for"),
267:                 "priority": r.get::<i32, _>("priority"),
268:                 "running": r.get::<bool, _>("running"),
269:                 "worker_id": r.get::<Option<String>, _>("worker_id"),
270:             })
271:         }).collect())
272:     }
273: 
274:     // ── Run operations ──
275: 
276:     pub async fn record_run(&self, id: &str, target: &str, kind: &str, args: &serde_json::Value) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
277:         sqlx::query(
278:             "INSERT INTO runs (id, target_path, kind, args) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4)",
279:         )
280:         .bind(id)
281:         .bind(target)
282:         .bind(kind)
283:         .bind(args)
284:         .execute(&self.pool)
285:         .await?;
286:         Ok(())
287:     }
288: 
289:     pub async fn update_run(&self, id: &str, state: &str, result: Option<&serde_json::Value>, error: Option<&str>, attempt: u32, duration_ms: i64) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
290:         sqlx::query(
291:             "UPDATE runs SET state = $1, result = $2, error = $3, attempt = $4, duration_ms = $5, completed_at = NOW() WHERE id = $6",
292:         )
293:         .bind(state)
294:         .bind(result)
295:         .bind(error)
296:         .bind(attempt as i32)
297:         .bind(duration_ms)
298:         .bind(id)
299:         .execute(&self.pool)
300:         .await?;
301:         Ok(())
302:     }
303: 
304:     pub async fn get_runs(&self, target: &str, limit: i64) -> Result<Vec<serde_json::Value>, sqlx::Error> {
305:         let rows = sqlx::query(
306:             "SELECT id, target_path, kind, state, attempt, error, duration_ms, created_at, completed_at
307:              FROM runs WHERE target_path = $1 ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $2",
308:         )
309:         .bind(target)
310:         .bind(limit)
311:         .fetch_all(&self.pool)
312:         .await?;
313:         Ok(rows.into_iter().map(|r| {
314:             serde_json::json!({
315:                 "id": r.get::<String, _>("id"),
316:                 "target": r.get::<String, _>("target_path"),
317:                 "kind": r.get::<String, _>("kind"),
318:                 "state": r.get::<String, _>("state"),

581:     async fn register_module(&self, path: &str, source: &str, manifest: &automaton_core::AutomationManifest) -> automaton_core::Result<automaton_core::ModuleId> {
582:         let manifest_val = serde_json::to_value(manifest).unwrap_or_default();
583:         let deps: Vec<automaton_core::DepRef> = manifest.depends_on.clone();
584:         self.register_script(path, source, &manifest.version, &manifest_val, &deps).await
585:             .map_err(|e| automaton_core::AutomatonError::Database(e.to_string()))?;
586:         let hash = automaton_core::ContentHash::compute(source.as_bytes());
587:         let version = semver::Version::parse(&manifest.version)
588:             .map_err(|e| automaton_core::AutomatonError::Other(e.to_string()))?;
589:         Ok(automaton_core::ModuleId {
590:             path: path.to_string(),
591:             version,
592:             hash,
593:             created_at: chrono::Utc::now(),
594:         })
595:     }

597:     async fn get_module(&self, path: &str) -> automaton_core::Result<Option<automaton_core::AutomationModule>> {
598:         let script = self.get_script(path).await
599:             .map_err(|e| automaton_core::AutomatonError::Database(e.to_string()))?;
600:         match script {
601:             Some(v) => {
602:                 let source = v.get("source").and_then(|s| s.as_str()).unwrap_or("").to_string();
603:                 let hash_str = v.get("hash").and_then(|h| h.as_str()).unwrap_or("");
604:                 let manifest_val = v.get("manifest").cloned().unwrap_or_default();
605:                 let manifest: automaton_core::AutomationManifest = serde_json::from_value(manifest_val)
606:                     .unwrap_or_else(|_| automaton_core::AutomationManifest {
607:                         name: path.to_string(),
608:                         version: "0.1.0".to_string(),
609:                         entry: "main".to_string(),
610:                         summary: None,
611:                         description: None,
612:                         timeout_ms: 30_000,
613:                         retry: None,
614:                         permissions: vec![],
615:                         depends_on: vec![],
616:                         resources: vec![],
617:                         tags: vec![],
618:                         require_approval: false,
619:                         inputs_schema: automaton_core::SchemaMode::Auto,
620:                         outputs_schema: automaton_core::SchemaMode::Auto,
621:                     });
622:                 let built = v.get("built").and_then(|b| b.as_bool()).unwrap_or(false);
623:                 Ok(Some(automaton_core::AutomationModule {
624:                     source,
625:                     hash: automaton_core::ContentHash(hash_str.to_string()),
626:                     manifest,
627:                     built,
628:                 }))

<content>
1: //! REST API server for Automaton — Axum-based HTTP interface.
2: //! Exposes all CRUD operations via JSON API, backed by automaton-postgres (sqlx).
3: 
4: use std::path::PathBuf;
5: use std::sync::Arc;
6: 
7: use axum::{
8:     extract::{Path, Query, State},
9:     http::StatusCode,
10:     middleware,
11:     response::{IntoResponse, Response},
12:     routing::{get, post},
13:     Json, Router,
14: };
15: use serde_json::Value;
16: 
17: use automaton_core::DepRef;
18: 
19: /// JWT auth middleware. If `AUTOMATON_JWT_SECRET` env var is set, validates
20: /// Bearer tokens on all `/api/*` routes. If unset, allows all requests.
21: use axum::body::Body;
22: 
23: async fn auth_middleware(
24:     request: axum::http::Request<Body>,
25:     next: middleware::Next,
26: ) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {
27:     let jwt_secret = std::env::var("AUTOMATON_JWT_SECRET").unwrap_or_default();
28:     if jwt_secret.is_empty() {
29:         return Ok(next.run(request).await);
30:     }
31: 
32:     let auth_header = request
33:         .headers()
34:         .get("Authorization")
35:         .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
36:         .and_then(|v| v.strip_prefix("Bearer "))
37:         .map(|s| s.to_string());
38:     let token = auth_header.ok_or(StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED)?;
39: 
40:     use jsonwebtoken::{decode, DecodingKey, Validation, Algorithm};
41:     let key = DecodingKey::from_secret(jwt_secret.as_bytes());
42:     let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);
43:     validation.validate_exp = true;
44:     decode::<serde_json::Value>(&token, &key, &validation)
45:         .map_err(|_| StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED)?;
46: 
47:     Ok(next.run(request).await)
48: }

50: /// Create a router with all API endpoints, backed by the given DB and build cache dir.
51: pub fn create_router(
52:     db: Arc<automaton_postgres::AutomatonDb>,
53:     data_dir: PathBuf,
54: ) -> Router {
55:     let build_cache_dir = data_dir.join("builds");
56:     let app_state = AppState { db, build_cache_dir, data_dir };
57: 
58:     Router::new()
59:         .route("/health", get(health_handler))
60:         .route("/api/scripts", get(list_scripts).post(create_script))
61:         .route("/api/scripts/:path", get(get_script))
62:         .route("/api/scripts/:path/build", post(build_script))
63:         .route("/api/scripts/:path/run", post(run_script))
64:         .route("/api/jobs", get(list_jobs).post(enqueue_job))
65:         .route("/api/runs", get(list_runs))
66:         .route("/api/variables", get(list_variables).post(set_variable))
67:         .route("/api/variables/:path", get(get_variable))
68:         .route("/api/resources", get(list_resources).post(set_resource))
69:         .route("/api/resources/:path", get(get_resource))
70:         .route("/api/webhooks/:trigger_id", post(webhook_handler))
71:         .route("/api/events/:trigger_id", post(event_handler))
72:         .route("/api/triggers", get(list_triggers).post(create_trigger))
73:         .route("/api/graph/nodes", post(add_node))
74:         .route("/api/graph/edges", post(add_edge))
75:         .route_layer(middleware::from_fn(auth_middleware))
76:         .with_state(Arc::new(app_state))
77: }

79: /// Start the API server on the given address.
80: pub async fn serve(
81:     addr: &str,
82:     database_url: &str,
83:     data_dir: Option<PathBuf>,
84: ) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
85:     let db = automaton_postgres::AutomatonDb::connect(database_url).await?;
86:     let db = Arc::new(db);
87:     let data_dir = data_dir.unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from("./data"));
88:     let app = create_router(db, data_dir);
89: 
90:     let listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(addr).await?;
91:     tracing::info!("Automaton API server starting on {addr}");
92:     axum::serve(listener, app).await?;
93:     Ok(())
94: }

123: async fn create_script(
124:     State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>,
125:     Json(body): Json<Value>,
126: ) -> Response {
127:     let path = body["path"].as_str().unwrap_or_default().to_string();
128:     let source = body["source"].as_str().unwrap_or_default().to_string();
129:     if path.is_empty() || source.is_empty() {
130:         return err_msg(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, "path and source are required");
131:     }
132:     let manifest = serde_json::json!({
133:         "name": path,
134:         "version": body.get("version").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("0.1.0"),
135:         "summary": body.get("summary"),
136:         "timeout_ms": body.get("timeout_ms").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(30000),
137:     });
138:     let deps: Vec<DepRef> = body.get("depends_on")
139:         .and_then(|d| d.as_array())
140:         .map(|arr| arr.iter().filter_map(|v| v.as_str()).map(DepRef::new).collect())
141:         .unwrap_or_default();
142: 
143:     match state.db.register_script(&path, &source, "0.1.0", &manifest, &deps).await {
144:         Ok(hash) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"hash": hash, "path": path})),
145:         Err(e) => err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
146:     }
147: }
148: 
149: async fn list_scripts(State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>) -> Response {
150:     match state.db.list_scripts().await {
151:         Ok(scripts) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"scripts": scripts})),
152:         Err(e) => err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
153:     }
154: }
155: 
156: async fn get_script(
157:     State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>,
158:     Path(path): Path<String>,
159: ) -> Response {
160:     match state.db.get_script(&path).await {
161:         Ok(Some(script)) => ok_json(script),
162:         Ok(None) => err_msg(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, "Script not found"),
163:         Err(e) => err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
164:     }
165: }
166: 
167: async fn build_script(
168:     State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>,
169:     Path(path): Path<String>,
170: ) -> Response {
171:     // Look up the script source
172:     let script = match state.db.get_script(&path).await {
173:         Ok(Some(s)) => s,
174:         Ok(None) => return err_msg(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, "Script not found"),
175:         Err(e) => return err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
176:     };
177: 
178:     let source = script.get("source").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("");
179:     if source.is_empty() {
180:         return err_msg(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, "Script has no source code");
181:     }
182: 
183:     // Build manifest from script metadata
184:     let manifest = automaton_core::AutomationManifest {
185:         name: path.clone(),
186:         version: script.get("version").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("0.1.0").to_string(),
187:         entry: "main".to_string(),
188:         summary: None,
189:         description: None,
190:         timeout_ms: 30_000,
191:         retry: None,
192:         permissions: vec![],
193:         depends_on: vec![],
194:         resources: vec![],
195:         tags: vec![],
196:         require_approval: false,
197:         inputs_schema: automaton_core::SchemaMode::Auto,
198:         outputs_schema: automaton_core::SchemaMode::Auto,
199:     };
200: 
201:     // Build using cache
202:     let build_cache = automaton_build::BuildCache::new(&state.data_dir);
203:     match build_cache.build_rust(&path, source, &manifest) {
204:         Ok((hash, binary_path)) => {
205:             ok_json(serde_json::json!({
206:                 "status": "built",
207:                 "hash": hash,
208:                 "binary": binary_path.to_string_lossy(),
209:             }))
210:         }
211:         Err(e) => {
212:             // Parse diagnostics for structured error feedback
213:             let diagnostics = automaton_build::BuildCache::diagnose(&e);
214:             ok_json(serde_json::json!({
215:                 "status": "failed",
216:                 "error": e,
217:                 "diagnostics": diagnostics,
218:             }))
219:         }
220:     }
221: }

229:     match state.db.enqueue("script", &path, &args).await {

368: /// Receive an incoming event and enqueue a job for the matching event trigger.
369: /// Validates that the trigger is an Event type. The body is passed as args.
370: async fn event_handler(
371:     State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>,
372:     Path(trigger_id): Path<String>,
373:     Json(args): Json<serde_json::Value>,
374: ) -> Response {
375:     let trigger = match state.db.get_trigger_by_id(&trigger_id).await {
376:         Ok(Some(t)) => t,
377:         Ok(None) => return err_msg(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, "Trigger not found"),
378:         Err(e) => return err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
379:     };
380: 
381:     if trigger.get("enabled") != Some(&serde_json::json!(true)) {
382:         return err_msg(StatusCode::GONE, "Trigger is disabled");
383:     }
384:     if trigger.get("trigger_type").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) != Some("event") {
385:         return err_msg(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, "Not an event trigger");
386:     }
387: 
388:     // Verify event_source match if configured on the trigger
389:     let expected_source = trigger.get("config")
390:         .and_then(|c| c.get("event_source"))
391:         .and_then(|v| v.as_str());
392:     if let Some(expected) = expected_source {
393:         let received = args.get("event_source").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("");
394:         if received != expected {
395:             return err_msg(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, "Event source mismatch");
396:         }
397:     }
398: 
399:     let target_path = trigger.get("target_path").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("");
400:     let target_is_flow = trigger.get("target_is_flow").and_then(|v| v.as_bool()).unwrap_or(false);
401:     let kind = if target_is_flow { "flow" } else { "script" };
402: 
403:     match state.db.enqueue(kind, target_path, &args).await {
404:         Ok(job_id) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({
405:             "status": "event_queued",
406:             "job_id": job_id,
407:             "target": target_path,
408:         })),
409:         Err(e) => err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
410:     }
411: }
412: 
413: /// Receive an incoming webhook event and enqueue a job for the matching trigger.
414: /// The body is parsed as JSON and passed as args to the target automation.
415: /// If the trigger's config has `webhook_secret`, pass it as `webhook_secret` in the JSON body.
416: async fn webhook_handler(
417:     State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>,
418:     Path(trigger_id): Path<String>,
419:     Json(args): Json<serde_json::Value>,
420: ) -> Response {
421:     // Look up trigger by ID
422:     let trigger = match state.db.get_trigger_by_id(&trigger_id).await {
423:         Ok(Some(t)) => t,
424:         Ok(None) => return err_msg(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, "Trigger not found"),
425:         Err(e) => return err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
426:     };
427: 
428:     if trigger.get("enabled") != Some(&serde_json::json!(true)) {
429:         return err_msg(StatusCode::GONE, "Trigger is disabled");
430:     }
431: 
432:     // Verify webhook secret if configured
433:     let expected_secret = trigger.get("config")
434:         .and_then(|c| c.get("webhook_secret"))
435:         .and_then(|v| v.as_str());
436:     if let Some(expected) = expected_secret {
437:         let received = args.get("webhook_secret").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("");
438:         if received != expected {
439:             return err_msg(StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED, "Invalid webhook secret");
440:         }
441:     }
442: 
443:     let target_path = trigger.get("target_path").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("");
444:     let target_is_flow = trigger.get("target_is_flow").and_then(|v| v.as_bool()).unwrap_or(false);
445:     let kind = if target_is_flow { "flow" } else { "script" };
446: 
447:     match state.db.enqueue(kind, target_path, &args).await {
448:         Ok(job_id) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({
449:             "status": "queued",
450:             "job_id": job_id,
451:             "target": target_path,
452:         })),
453:         Err(e) => err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
454:     }
455: }
456: 
457: async fn list_triggers(State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>) -> Response {
458:     // Return all triggers regardless of type
459:     let cron = state.db.get_enabled_triggers("cron").await.unwrap_or_default();
460:     let webhook = state.db.get_enabled_triggers("webhook").await.unwrap_or_default();
461:     let event = state.db.get_enabled_triggers("event").await.unwrap_or_default();
462: 
463:     let mut all = cron;
464:     all.extend(webhook);
465:     all.extend(event);
466:     all.sort_by(|a, b| {
467:         a.get("created_at")
468:             .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
469:             .unwrap_or("")
470:             .cmp(b.get("created_at").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or(""))
471:     });
472: 
473:     ok_json(serde_json::json!({"triggers": all}))
474: }

1: use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
2: 
3: /// A stored secret/variable — encrypted at rest
4: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
5: pub struct Variable {
6:     /// e.g. "slack/api_token" — can be referenced as $var:slack/api_token
7:     pub path: String,
8:     /// Encrypted value (the runtime decrypts before injection)

18: pub struct Resource {

20:     pub path: String,
21:     /// e.g. "postgresql", "slack", "github"
22:     pub resource_type: String,
23:     /// Connection config
24:     pub value: serde_json::Value,
25:     pub description: Option<String>,
26:     pub created_at: chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>,
27: }
28:

31: pub struct ResourceType {
32:     pub name: String,
33:     pub schema: serde_json::Value,
34: }
35: 
36: impl ResourceType {
37:     pub fn builtin_types() -> Vec<Self> {
38:         vec![
39:             Self {
40:                 name: "postgresql".into(),
41:                 schema: serde_json::json!({"type":"object"}),
42:             },
43:             Self {
44:                 name: "slack".into(),
45:                 schema: serde_json::json!({"type":"object"}),
46:             },
47:             Self {
48:                 name: "github".into(),
49:                 schema: serde_json::json!({"type":"object"}),
50:             },
51:             Self {
52:                 name: "openai".into(),
53:                 schema: serde_json::json!({"type":"object"}),
54:             },
55:             Self {
56:                 name: "anthropic".into(),
57:                 schema: serde_json::json!({"type":"object"}),
58:             },
59:             Self {
60:                 name: "http".into(),
61:                 schema: serde_json::json!({"type":"object"}),
62:             },
63:             Self {
64:                 name: "aws".into(),
65:                 schema: serde_json::json!({"type":"object"}),
66:             },
67:         ]
68:     }
69: }
70: 
71: /// AES-256-GCM encryption/decryption for secret values.
72: /// Master key is read from AUTOMATON_MASTER_KEY environment variable.
73: pub struct SecretKeeper {
74:     key: [u8; 32],
75: }
76: 
77: impl SecretKeeper {
78:     /// Create a new SecretKeeper, reading the master key from AUTOMATON_MASTER_KEY.
79:     /// If the env var is not set, a deterministic key is derived from "automaton-default-key".
80:     pub fn from_env() -> Self {
81:         let key_hex = std::env::var("AUTOMATON_MASTER_KEY").unwrap_or_default();
82:         let key = if key_hex.len() == 64 {
83:             let mut k = [0u8; 32];
84:             if hex::decode_to_slice(key_hex.as_bytes(), &mut k).is_err() {
85:                 // Fallback on decode failure
86:                 use sha2::Digest;
87:                 let hash = sha2::Sha256::digest(b"automaton-fallback-key");
88:                 k.copy_from_slice(&hash);
89:             }
90:             k
91:         } else {
92:             let mut k = [0u8; 32];
93:             use sha2::Digest;
94:             let hash = sha2::Sha256::digest(b"automaton-fallback-key");
95:             k.copy_from_slice(&hash);
96:             k
97:         };
98:         Self { key }
99:     }
100: 
101:     /// Create a SecretKeeper with a specific key (for testing)
102:     pub fn with_key(key: [u8; 32]) -> Self {
103:         Self { key }
104:     }
105: 
106:     /// Encrypt a plaintext value.
107:     /// Returns base64(nonce || ciphertext).
108:     pub fn encrypt(&self, plaintext: &str) -> String {
109:         use aes_gcm::aead::{Aead, KeyInit};
110:         use aes_gcm::{Aes256Gcm, Nonce};
111:         let cipher = Aes256Gcm::new_from_slice(&self.key).expect("valid key");
112:         let mut nonce = [0u8; 12];
113:         use rand::RngCore;
114:         rand::rngs::OsRng.fill_bytes(&mut nonce);
115:         let ciphertext = cipher
116:             .encrypt(Nonce::from_slice(&nonce), plaintext.as_bytes())
117:             .expect("encryption failed");
118:         let mut combined = nonce.to_vec();
119:         combined.extend_from_slice(&ciphertext);
120:         base64::Engine::encode(&base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD, &combined)
121:     }
122: 
123:     /// Decrypt a value previously encrypted with `encrypt`.
124:     /// Input: base64(nonce || ciphertext).
125:     pub fn decrypt(&self, encrypted: &str) -> Option<String> {
126:         use aes_gcm::aead::{Aead, KeyInit};
127:         use aes_gcm::{Aes256Gcm, Nonce};
128:         let combined = base64::Engine::decode(
129:             &base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD,
130:             encrypted.as_bytes(),
131:         )
132:         .ok()?;
133:         if combined.len() < 12 {
134:             return None;
135:         }
136:         let (nonce_bytes, ciphertext) = combined.split_at(12);
137:         let cipher = Aes256Gcm::new_from_slice(&self.key).ok()?;
138:         let plaintext = cipher
139:             .decrypt(Nonce::from_slice(nonce_bytes), ciphertext)
140:             .ok()?;
141:         String::from_utf8(plaintext).ok()
142:     }
143: }

17: /// Automaton — AI-native Rust automation substrate.
18: ///
19: /// A CLI-based framework for AI agents to create, compose, and execute
20: /// modular Rust automations backed by a property graph and MCP-native control.
21: #[derive(Parser)]
22: #[command(name = "automaton", version, about, long_about = None)]
23: struct Cli {
24:     #[command(subcommand)]
25:     command: Commands,
26: }
27: 
28: #[derive(Subcommand)]
29: enum Commands {
30:     /// Initialize a new automaton workspace
31:     Init,
32: 
33:     /// Scaffold a new automation module
34:     New {
35:         /// Module path, e.g. "github.issue_triage"
36:         path: String,
37:         /// Template pattern (echo, http-fetch, http-server, db-query, slack-notify, data-transform, health-check, rate-limiter, file-watch, cron-worker)
38:         #[arg(long, default_value = "echo")]
39:         pattern: String,
40:     },
41: 
42:     /// Build a module into a compiled binary
43:     Build {
44:         /// Module path
45:         path: String,
46:         /// Build mode (debug/release)
47:         #[arg(long, default_value = "debug")]
48:         mode: String,
49:     },
50: 
51:     /// Run a module locally
52:     Run {
53:         /// Module path
54:         path: String,
55:         /// JSON input string
56:         #[arg(long)]
57:         input: Option<String>,
58:     },
59: 
60:     /// Inspect and query the design graph
61:     Graph {
62:         #[command(subcommand)]
63:         action: GraphCommand,
64:     },
65: 
66:     /// Plan a workflow from a module
67:     Plan {
68:         /// Starting module path
69:         start: String,
70:         /// Maximum dependency depth
71:         #[arg(long, default_value = "10")]
72:         max_depth: usize,
73:     },
74: 
75:     /// Plan and execute a workflow from a module
76:     Execute {
77:         /// Module path to plan and execute from
78:         module: String,
79:         /// Maximum dependency depth
80:         #[arg(long, default_value = "10")]
81:         max_depth: usize,
82:         /// JSON input to pass to the first module (optional)
83:         #[arg(long)]
84:         input: Option<String>,
85:     },
86: 
87:     /// Start the MCP server (stdio transport)
88:     Mcp,
89: 
90:     /// List registered modules
91:     List {
92:         /// Filter by query
93:         query: Option<String>,
94:     },
95: 
96:     /// Show module details
97:     Show {
98:         /// Module path
99:         path: String,
100:     },
101: 
102:     /// View run logs
103:     Logs {
104:         /// Module path filter
105:         module: Option<String>,
106:         /// Max results
107:         #[arg(long, default_value = "20")]
108:         limit: usize,
109:     },
110: 
111:     /// Retry a failed run
112:     Retry {
113:         /// Run ID
114:         run_id: String,
115:     },
116: 
117:     /// Start the worker daemon (processes queued jobs)
118:     Worker {
119:         /// Worker name (for multi-worker setups)
120:         #[arg(long, default_value = "default")]
121:         name: String,
122:         /// Number of concurrent jobs
123:         #[arg(long, default_value = "4")]
124:         concurrency: usize,
125:         /// Poll interval in milliseconds
126:         #[arg(long, default_value = "5000")]
127:         poll_interval_ms: u64,
128:         /// Detach and run in background (writes PID to data dir)
129:         #[arg(long)]
130:         daemon: bool,
131:     },
132: 
133:     /// Run system diagnostics
134:     Doctor,
135: }
136: 
137: #[derive(Subcommand)]
138: enum GraphCommand {
139:     /// List all nodes
140:     Nodes,
141:     /// List all edges
142:     Edges,
143:     /// Find a path between two nodes
144:     Path {
145:         from: String,
146:         to: String,
147:     },
148:     /// Show dependency chain for a node
149:     Deps {
150:         node_id: String,
151:     },
152: }

154: // ── TriggerProvider adapter for Registry ──
155: 
156: struct RegistryTriggerProvider {
157:     registry: Registry,
158: }
159: 
160: #[async_trait::async_trait]
161: impl TriggerProvider for RegistryTriggerProvider {
162:     async fn get_cron_triggers(&self) -> std::result::Result<Vec<ScheduledTrigger>, String> {
163:         let triggers = self.registry.get_enabled_triggers("cron")
164:             .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
165:         let mut result = Vec::new();
166:         for t in triggers {
167:             let config = t.get("config").and_then(|c| c.as_object()).cloned().unwrap_or_default();
168:             let schedule = config.get("schedule")
169:                 .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
170:                 .unwrap_or("")
171:                 .to_string();
172:             if schedule.is_empty() {
173:                 continue;
174:             }
175:             result.push(ScheduledTrigger {
176:                 id: t.get("id").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("").to_string(),
177:                 target_path: t.get("target_path").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("").to_string(),
178:                 target_is_flow: t.get("target_is_flow").and_then(|v| v.as_bool()).unwrap_or(false),
179:                 schedule,
180:                 args: config.get("args").cloned(),
181:             });
182:         }
183:         Ok(result)
184:     }
185: 
186:     async fn enqueue_job(&self, kind: &str, target: &str, args: &serde_json::Value) -> std::result::Result<i64, String> {
187:         self.registry.enqueue(kind, target, args)
188:             .map_err(|e| e.to_string())
189:     }
190: }
191: 
192: fn default_data_dir() -> PathBuf {
193:     dirs::data_dir()
194:         .unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from(".local/share"))
195:         .join("automaton")
196: }
197: 
198: fn init_engine(data_dir: &PathBuf) -> Result<Engine> {
199:     std::fs::create_dir_all(data_dir)?;
200:     let registry = Registry::open(data_dir)?;
201:     // Use merged registry.db for graph store (replaces separate graph.db)
202:     let graph_store = GraphStore::open_merged(data_dir)?;
203:     let runtime_config = RuntimeConfig {
204:         work_dir: data_dir.join("work"),
205:         temp_dir: data_dir.join("tmp"),
206:         ..Default::default()
207:     };
208:     let runtime = Runtime::new(runtime_config);
209:     Ok(Engine::new(Arc::new(registry), graph_store, runtime))
210: }
211: 
212: #[tokio::main]
213: async fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {
214:     tracing_subscriber::fmt()
215:         .with_env_filter(tracing_subscriber::EnvFilter::from_default_env()
216:             .add_directive("automaton=info".parse()?))
217:         .init();
218: 
219:     let cli = Cli::parse();
220:     let data_dir = default_data_dir();
221: 
222:     match cli.command {
223:         Commands::Init => {
224:             std::fs::create_dir_all(&data_dir)?;
225:             std::fs::create_dir_all(data_dir.join("modules"))?;
226:             std::fs::create_dir_all(data_dir.join("builds"))?;
227:             std::fs::create_dir_all(data_dir.join("work"))?;
228:             std::fs::create_dir_all(data_dir.join("tmp"))?;
229: 
230:             println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
231:                 "status": "initialized",
232:                 "data_dir": data_dir.to_string_lossy(),
233:                 "version": env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"),
234:             }))?);
235:         }
236: 
237:         Commands::New { path, pattern } => {
238:             if path.is_empty() || path.trim().is_empty() {
239:                 anyhow::bail!("Module path cannot be empty");
240:             }
241:             if path.contains("..") || path.contains("//") {
242:                 anyhow::bail!("Invalid module path: {path}");
243:             }
244:             let module_dir = data_dir.join("modules").join(path.replace('.', "/"));
245:             std::fs::create_dir_all(&module_dir)?;
246:             let safe = path.replace('.', "_");
247:             // Check for template pattern
248:             let source = if let Some(tmpl) = automaton_build::templates::get_template(&pattern) {
249:                 tmpl.source.to_string()
250:             } else {
251:                 // Fallback: simple echo source
252:                 format!("// Automation: {safe}\nfn main() {{\n    let msg = serde_json::json!({{ \"status\": \"ok\", \"module\": \"{safe}\" }});\n    println!(\"{{}}\", msg);\n}}\n")
253:             };
254:             let source_path = module_dir.join("main.rs");
255:             std::fs::write(&source_path, &source)?;
256: 
257:             let manifest = AutomationManifest {
258:                 name: path.clone(),
259:                 version: "0.1.0".to_string(),
260:                 entry: "main".to_string(),
261:                 summary: Some(format!("Automation: {path}")),
262:                 description: None,
263:                 timeout_ms: 30_000,
264:                 ..Default::default()
265:             };
266:             let yaml = serde_yaml::to_string(&manifest)?;
267:             let yaml_path = module_dir.join("automation.yaml");
268:             std::fs::write(&yaml_path, &yaml)?;
269: 
270:             // Register module in registry + add to graph (single engine init)
271:             match init_engine(&data_dir) {
272:                 Ok(engine) => {
273:                     match engine.backend().register_module(&path, &source, &manifest).await {
274:                         Ok(_id) => {
275:                             let mut props = std::collections::HashMap::new();
276:                             props.insert("path".into(), serde_json::json!(path));
277:                             let _ = engine.graph().add_node(NodeKind::Module, &path, props);
278:                         }
279:                         Err(e) => eprintln!("Warning: Failed to register module: {e}"),
280:                     }
281:                 }
282:                 Err(e) => eprintln!("Warning: Failed to init engine: {e}"),
283:             }
284: 
285:             println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
286:                 "status": "created",
287:                 "path": path,
288:                 "source": source_path.to_string_lossy(),
289:                 "manifest": yaml_path.to_string_lossy(),
290:             }))?);
291:         }
292: 
293:         Commands::Build { path, mode } => {
294:             let engine = init_engine(&data_dir)?;
295:             let module = engine.backend().get_module(&path).await?
296:                 .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("Module not found: {path}"))?;
297: 
298:             let build_cache = automaton_build::BuildCache::new(&data_dir);
299:             let (hash, binary_path) = build_cache.build_rust(
300:                 &path,
301:                 &module.source,
302:                 &module.manifest,
303:             ).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Build failed: {e}"))?;
304: 
305:             engine.backend().mark_built(&path).await?;
306:             engine.backend().record_build(&hash, &binary_path.to_string_lossy(), &mode).await?;
307: 
308:             println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
309:                 "status": "built",
310:                 "path": path,
311:                 "mode": mode,
312:                 "binary": binary_path.to_string_lossy(),
313:                 "hash": hash,
314:             }))?);
315:         }
316: 
317:         Commands::Run { path, input } => {
318:             let engine = init_engine(&data_dir)?;
319:             let module = engine.backend().get_module(&path).await?
320:                 .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("Module not found: {path}"))?;
321: 
322:             if !module.built {
323:                 println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
324:                     "status": "skipped",
325:                     "reason": "Module not built yet. Run `automaton build {path}` first.",
326:                 }))?);
327:                 return Ok(());
328:             }
329: 
330:             let input_value: serde_json::Value = if let Some(input_str) = input {
331:                 serde_json::from_str(&input_str)?
332:             } else {
333:                 serde_json::json!({})
334:             };
335: 
336:             let binary_path = engine.backend().build_cache_dir()
337:                 .join(path.replace('.', "_"));
338:             let runtime = Runtime::new(RuntimeConfig::default());
339:             let result = runtime.run_binary(&binary_path, &input_value, module.manifest.timeout_ms).await;
340: 
341:             match result {
342:                 Ok(output) => {
343:                     println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
344:                         "status": "completed",
345:                         "output": output,
346:                     }))?);
347:                 }
348:                 Err(e) => {
349:                     println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
350:                         "status": "failed",
351:                         "error": e.to_string(),
352:                     }))?);
353:                 }
354:             }
355:         }
356: 
357:         Commands::Graph { action } => {
358:             let engine = init_engine(&data_dir)?;
359: 
360:             match action {
361:                 GraphCommand::Nodes => {
362:                     let nodes = engine.graph().all_nodes()?;
363:                     println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
364:                         "count": nodes.len(),
365:                         "nodes": nodes.iter().map(|n| serde_json::json!({
366:                             "id": n.id,
367:                             "name": n.name,
368:                             "kind": format!("{:?}", n.kind),
369:                             "properties": n.properties,
370:                         })).collect::<Vec<_>>(),
371:                     }))?);
372:                 }
373:                 GraphCommand::Edges => {
374:                     let edges = engine.graph().all_edges()?;
375:                     println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
376:                         "count": edges.len(),
377:                         "edges": edges.iter().map(|e| serde_json::json!({
378:                             "id": e.id,
379:                             "source": e.source,
380:                             "target": e.target,
381:                             "kind": format!("{:?}", e.kind),
382:                         })).collect::<Vec<_>>(),
383:                     }))?);
384:                 }
385:                 GraphCommand::Path { from, to } => {
386:                     let paths = engine.graph().find_path(&from, &to)?;
387:                     println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
388:                         "paths_found": paths.len(),
389:                         "paths": paths.iter().map(|path| {
390:                             path.iter().map(|na| serde_json::json!({
391:                                 "node": na.node.name,
392:                                 "edge": format!("{:?}", na.edge_kind),
393:                             })).collect::<Vec<_>>()
394:                         }).collect::<Vec<_>>(),
395:                     }))?);
396:                 }
397:                 GraphCommand::Deps { node_id } => {
398:                     let chain = engine.graph().get_dependency_chain(&node_id)?;
399:                     println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
400:                         "count": chain.len(),
401:                         "chain": chain.iter().map(|n| serde_json::json!({
402:                             "id": n.id,
403:                             "name": n.name,
404:                             "kind": format!("{:?}", n.kind),
405:                         })).collect::<Vec<_>>(),
406:                     }))?);
407:                 }
408:             }
409:         }
410: 
411:         Commands::Plan { start, max_depth } => {
412:             let engine = init_engine(&data_dir)?;
413:             let options = PlanOptions { max_depth, ..Default::default() };
414:             let run_graph = engine.plan(&start, &options).await?;
415: 
416:             println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
417:                 "run_graph_id": run_graph.id,
418:                 "workflow": run_graph.workflow_name,
419:                 "modules": run_graph.modules.iter().map(|m| serde_json::json!({
420:                     "id": m.id,
421:                     "module": m.module_id.path,
422:                     "depends_on": m.depends_on,
423:                     "retry": serde_json::to_value(&m.retry).ok(),
424:                     "timeout_ms": m.timeout_ms,
425:                 })).collect::<Vec<_>>(),
426:                 "total_modules": run_graph.modules.len(),
427:             }))?);
428:         }
429: 
430:         Commands::Execute { module, max_depth, input: _ } => {
431:             let engine = init_engine(&data_dir)?;
432: 
433:             // Plan from the module path
434:             let options = PlanOptions { max_depth, ..Default::default() };
435:             let run_graph = engine.plan(&module, &options).await?;
436:             let dag = engine.materialize(&run_graph)?;
437: 
438:             let result = engine.execute(dag).await?;
439:             println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
440:                 "status": "executed",
441:                 "run_id": result.run_graph_id,
442:                 "results": result.results,
443:             }))?);
444:         }
445: 
446:         Commands::Mcp => {
447:             let engine = init_engine(&data_dir)?;
448:             let server = McpServer::new(engine, data_dir);
449:             tracing::info!("Starting Automaton MCP server on stdio");
450:             server.serve_stdio().await?;
451:         }
452: 
453:         Commands::List { query } => {
454:             let engine = init_engine(&data_dir)?;
455:             let all = engine.backend().list_modules().await?;
456:             let filtered: Vec<_> = if let Some(q) = query {
457:                 all.into_iter().filter(|(p, _, _, _)| p.contains(&q)).collect()
458:             } else {
459:                 all
460:             };
461: 
462:             println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
463:                 "count": filtered.len(),
464:                 "modules": filtered.iter().map(|(path, ver, hash, built)| serde_json::json!({
465:                     "path": path,
466:                     "version": ver,
467:                     "hash": hash,
468:                     "built": built,
469:                 })).collect::<Vec<_>>(),
470:             }))?);
471:         }
472: 
473:         Commands::Show { path } => {
474:             let engine = init_engine(&data_dir)?;
475:             match engine.backend().get_module(&path).await? {
476:                 Some(module) => {
477:                     println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
478:                         "path": module.manifest.name,
479:                         "version": module.manifest.version,
480:                         "hash": module.hash.as_str(),
481:                         "built": module.built,
482:                         "entry": module.manifest.entry,
483:                         "summary": module.manifest.summary,
484:                         "dependencies": module.manifest.depends_on.iter().map(|d| serde_json::json!({
485:                             "name": d.name,
486:                             "version_req": d.version_req,
487:                         })).collect::<Vec<_>>(),
488:                         "timeout_ms": module.manifest.timeout_ms,
489:                         "retry": serde_json::to_value(&module.manifest.retry).ok(),
490:                         "tags": module.manifest.tags,
491:                     }))?);
492:                 }
493:                 None => {
494:                     eprintln!("Module not found: {path}");
495:                     std::process::exit(1);
496:                 }
497:             }
498:         }
499: 
500:         Commands::Logs { module, limit } => {
501:             let engine = init_engine(&data_dir)?;
502:             let runs = if let Some(ref module_path) = module {
503:                 engine.backend().get_runs(module_path).await?
504:             } else {
505:                 vec![]
506:             };
507: 
508:             let truncated: Vec<_> = runs.into_iter().take(limit).collect();
509:             println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
510:                 "count": truncated.len(),
511:                 "runs": truncated,
512:             }))?);
513:         }
514: 
515:         Commands::Retry { run_id } => {
516:             println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
517:                 "status": "retry_scheduled",

519:             }))?);
520:         }
521: 
522:         Commands::Worker { name, concurrency, poll_interval_ms, daemon } => {
523:             // Open separate registry instances (SQLite handles concurrent access)
524:             let worker_registry = Registry::open(&data_dir)?;
525:             let scheduler_registry = Registry::open(&data_dir)?;
526: 
527:             let build_cache = BuildCache::new(&data_dir);
528:             let worker = Worker::new(&name, concurrency)
529:                 .with_build_cache(build_cache);
530: 
531:             // Start the scheduler daemon to fire cron triggers in the background
532:             let provider = Arc::new(RegistryTriggerProvider { registry: scheduler_registry });
533:             let _scheduler = SchedulerDaemon::start(provider, 60_000);
534: 
535:             if daemon {
536:                 let pid_path = data_dir.join("worker.pid");
537:                 let child = std::process::Command::new(std::env::current_exe()?)
538:                     .args([
539:                         "worker",
540:                         "--name", &name,
541:                         "--concurrency", &concurrency.to_string(),
542:                         "--poll-interval-ms", &poll_interval_ms.to_string(),
543:                     ])
544:                     .stdout(std::process::Stdio::null())
545:                     .stderr(std::process::Stdio::null())
546:                     .stdin(std::process::Stdio::null())
547:                     .spawn()?;
548:                 std::fs::write(&pid_path, child.id().to_string())?;
549:                 println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
550:                     "status": "worker_detached",
551:                     "name": name,
552:                     "pid": child.id(),
553:                     "pid_file": pid_path.to_string_lossy(),
554:                 }))?);
555:                 std::process::exit(0);
556:             }
557: 
558:             println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
559:                 "status": "worker_started",
560:                 "name": name,
561:                 "concurrency": concurrency,
562:                 "poll_interval_ms": poll_interval_ms,
563:                 "scheduler": "active",
564:             }))?);
565: 
566:             // Worker runs in the foreground, processing jobs from the queue
567:             worker.start(&worker_registry, poll_interval_ms).await;
568:         }
569: 
570:         Commands::Doctor => {
571:             let engine = init_engine(&data_dir)?;
572:             let module_count = engine.backend().list_modules().await.unwrap_or_default().len();
573:             let graph_nodes = engine.graph().all_nodes().unwrap_or_default().len();
574:             let graph_edges = engine.graph().all_edges().unwrap_or_default().len();
575: 
576:             println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
577:                 "status": "healthy",
578:                 "version": env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"),
579:                 "data_dir": data_dir.to_string_lossy(),
580:                 "components": {
581:                     "registry": {
582:                         "status": "ok",
583:                         "modules_registered": module_count,
584:                     },
585:                     "graph": {
586:                         "status": "ok",
587:                         "nodes": graph_nodes,
588:                         "edges": graph_edges,
589:                     },
590:                     "runtime": {
591:                         "status": "ready",
592:                         "max_concurrency": 4,
593:                     },
